title: Sidechain Compression
summary: Ducking your pads/bass in time with the kick for that pumping garage feel.
steps: Add a Compressor to the pad or synth track you want to duck
Click the triangle (sidechain) button in the Compressor header to expand the sidechain section
Enable the sidechain section by clicking the small power button next to 'Sidechain'
Set the 'Audio From' dropdown to your kick track (e.g. 'Drum Rack' or '1-Kick')
Set Ratio to 4:1, Attack to 0.1ms, Release to 100–200ms
Lower the Threshold until the pad ducks noticeably on each kick hit — watch the gain reduction meter
tips: Longer release = slower, breathier pump. Shorter = tighter, more mechanical duck
For subtle glue compression, use 2:1 ratio. For obvious garage pump, try 8:1 or higher
If the sidechain is too obvious, automate the dry/wet knob on the compressor to only activate on drops
Try routing a ghost kick (inaudible, volume all the way down) as the sidechain source for more precise triggering without drum bleed

images.caption: Click the small triangle labeled 'Sidechain' at the top-right of the Compressor to expand the panel. Click the power button to enable it. Set 'Audio From' to your DRUMS group track. Then lower the Threshold knob until the GR bar shows movement on every kick hit.
